pattern: Because values are small, indexing subsequence state by (ending value, last absolute difference) instead of by position turns the search into a compact table that only grows with the value range, not the array length.
hints: What matters going forward is only the value a subsequence currently ends on and the size of its last step, not which index it came from.

For every ending value w and every last-difference d, keep the longest subsequence achieving that combination; extending with a new number x of difference d from w requires the subsequence at w to have had a last difference of at least d.

Maintain, for each value w, a suffix-maximum over its difference table so 'best length with last diff >= d' is an O(1) lookup, then update the two values (x-d and x+d) that could feed into every difference d for the newly seen number.

edge_inputs.description: strictly decreasing gaps across three values
non-increasing gaps of two and one
repeated values keep the gap sequence valid at zero
minimum length, any pair trivially qualifies
all identical values, every step has difference zero
an increasing run only ever supports length two
extreme values at both ends of the range

stress.description: long random sequence over the full small value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 10000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 300) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
